 49→/**
 50→ * Formats the class signature according to https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html#class-headers
 51→ *
 52→ * In code style `ktlint_official` class headers containing 2 or more parameters are formatted as multiline signature. As the Kotlin Coding
 53→ * conventions do not specify what is meant with a "few parameters", no default is set for other code styles.
 54→ */
 55→public class ClassSignatureRule :
 56→    StandardRule(
 57→        id = "class-signature",
 58→        visitorModifiers =
 59→            setOf(
 60→                // Run after wrapping and spacing rules
 61→                RunAsLateAsPossible,
 62→                RunAfterRule(DISCOURAGED_COMMENT_LOCATION_RULE_ID, ONLY_WHEN_RUN_AFTER_RULE_IS_LOADED_AND_ENABLED),
 63→            ),
 64→        usesEditorConfigProperties =
 65→            setOf(
 66→                INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY,
 67→                INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY,
 68→                MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY,
 69→                FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY,
 70→            ),
 71→    ),
 72→    Rule.Experimental {
 73→    private var codeStyle = CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 74→    private var indentConfig = DEFAULT_INDENT_CONFIG
 75→    private var maxLineLength = MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 76→    private var classSignatureWrappingMinimumParameters = FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY.defaultValue
 77→
 78→    override fun beforeFirstNode(editorConfig: EditorConfig) {
 79→        codeStyle = editorConfig[CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY]
 80→        classSignatureWrappingMinimumParameters = editorConfig[FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY]
 81→        indentConfig =
 82→            IndentConfig(
 83→                indentStyle = editorConfig[INDENT_STYLE_PROPERTY],
 84→                tabWidth = editorConfig[INDENT_SIZE_PROPERTY],
 85→            )
 86→        maxLineLength = editorConfig[MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY]
 87→    }
 88→
 89→    override fun beforeVisitChildNodes(
 90→        node: ASTNode,
 91→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
 92→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
 93→    ) {
 94→        if (node.elementType == CLASS) {
 95→            visitClass(node, emit, autoCorrect)
 96→        }
 97→    }
 98→
 99→    private fun ASTNode.classSignatureNodes(excludeSuperTypes: Boolean): List<ASTNode> {
100→        // Find the nodes that are to be placed on the same line if no max line length is set
101→        //     internal class Foo(bar: String) { // Class without super type
102→        // or
103→        //     public class Foo(bar: String) : Bar { // Class with exactly one super type
104→        // or
105→        //     private class Foo(bar: String) : // Class with multiple super types which have to be wrapped to next lines
106→        val firstCodeChild = getFirstChildInSignature()
107→        val lastNodeInPrimaryClassSignatureLine =
108→            takeIf { excludeSuperTypes }
109→                // When the class extends multiple super types or if the super type list contains a newline, all super types are wrapped
110→                // on separate line
111→                ?.findChildByType(COLON)
112→                ?: findChildByType(CLASS_BODY)?.firstChildNode
113→        return collectLeavesRecursively()
114→            .childrenBetween(
115→                startASTNodePredicate = { it == firstCodeChild },
116→                endASTNodePredicate = { it == lastNodeInPrimaryClassSignatureLine },
117→            )
118→    }
119→
120→    private fun ASTNode.superTypes() =
121→        findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST)
122→            ?.children()
123→            ?.filterNot { it.isWhiteSpace() || it.isPartOfComment() || it.elementType == COMMA }
124→
125→    private fun ASTNode.hasMultilineSuperTypeList() = findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST)?.textContains('\n') == true
126→
127→    private fun ASTNode.getFirstChildInSignature(): ASTNode? {
128→        findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
129→            ?.let { modifierList ->
130→                val iterator = modifierList.children().iterator()
131→                var currentNode: ASTNode
132→                while (iterator.hasNext()) {
133→                    currentNode = iterator.next()
134→                    if (currentNode.elementType != ANNOTATION &&
135→                        currentNode.elementType != ANNOTATION_ENTRY &&
136→                        currentNode.elementType != WHITE_SPACE
137→                    ) {
138→                        return currentNode
139→                    }
140→                }
141→                return modifierList.nextCodeSibling()
142→            }
143→        return nextCodeLeaf()
144→    }
145→
146→    private fun visitClass(
147→        node: ASTNode,
148→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
149→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
150→    ) {
151→        require(node.elementType == CLASS)
152→
153→        val wrapPrimaryConstructorParameters =
154→            node.hasMinimumNumberOfParameters() ||
155→                node.containsMultilineParameter() ||
156→                (codeStyle == ktlint_official && node.containsAnnotatedParameter()) ||
157→                (isMaxLineLengthSet() && classSignatureExcludingSuperTypesExceedsMaxLineLength(node, emit, autoCorrect)) ||
158→                (!isMaxLineLengthSet() && node.classSignatureExcludingSuperTypesIsMultiline())
159→        fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = wrapPrimaryConstructorParameters, dryRun = false)
160→        fixWhitespacesInSuperTypeList(node, emit, autoCorrect, wrappedPrimaryConstructor = wrapPrimaryConstructorParameters)
161→        fixClassBody(node, emit, autoCorrect)
162→    }
163→
164→    private fun classSignatureExcludingSuperTypesExceedsMaxLineLength(
165→        node: ASTNode,
166→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
167→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
168→    ): Boolean {
169→        val actualClassSignatureLength = node.getClassSignatureLength(excludeSuperTypes = true)
170→        // Calculate the length of the class signature in case it, excluding the super types, would be rewritten as single
171→        // line (and without a maximum line length). The white space correction will be calculated via a dry run of the
172→        // actual fix.
173→        val length =
174→            actualClassSignatureLength +
175→                // Calculate the white space correction in case the signature would be rewritten to a single line
176→                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = true)
177→        return length > maxLineLength
178→    }
179→
180→    private fun ASTNode.classSignatureExcludingSuperTypesIsMultiline() =
181→        classSignatureNodes(excludeSuperTypes = true)
182→            .any { it.textContains('\n') }
183→
184→    private fun ASTNode.getClassSignatureLength(excludeSuperTypes: Boolean) =
185→        indent(false).length + getClassSignatureNodesLength(excludeSuperTypes)
186→
187→    private fun ASTNode.getClassSignatureNodesLength(excludeSuperTypes: Boolean) =
188→        classSignatureNodes(excludeSuperTypes)
189→            .joinTextToString()
190→            .length
191→
192→    private fun ASTNode.containsMultilineParameter(): Boolean =
193→        getPrimaryConstructorParameterListOrNull()
194→            ?.children()
195→            .orEmpty()
196→            .filter { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
197→            .any { it.textContains('\n') }
198→
199→    private fun ASTNode.containsAnnotatedParameter(): Boolean =
200→        getPrimaryConstructorParameterListOrNull()
201→            ?.children()
202→            .orEmpty()
203→            .filter { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
204→            .any { it.isAnnotated() }
205→
206→    private fun ASTNode.isAnnotated() =
207→        findChildByType(MODIFIER_LIST)
208→            ?.children()
209→            .orEmpty()
210→            .any { it.elementType == ANNOTATION_ENTRY }
211→
212→    private fun fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(
213→        node: ASTNode,
214→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
215→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
216→        multiline: Boolean,
217→        dryRun: Boolean,
218→    ): Int {
219→        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
220→
221→        val primaryConstructorParameterList = node.getPrimaryConstructorParameterListOrNull()
222→        val hasNoValueParameters =
223→            primaryConstructorParameterList
224→                ?.children()
225→                .orEmpty()
226→                .none { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
227→
228→        whiteSpaceCorrection +=
229→            if (hasNoValueParameters) {
230→                fixWhiteSpacesInEmptyValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, dryRun)
231→            } else {
232→                fixWhiteSpacesBeforeFirstParameterInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun) +
233→                    fixWhiteSpacesBetweenParametersInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun) +
234→                    fixWhiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline, dryRun)
235→            }
236→
237→        return whiteSpaceCorrection
238→    }
239→
240→    private fun fixWhiteSpacesInEmptyValueParameterList(
241→        node: ASTNode,
242→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
243→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
244→        dryRun: Boolean,
245→    ): Int {
246→        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
247→
248→        node
249→            .getPrimaryConstructorParameterListOrNull()
250→            ?.takeUnless { it.containsComment() }
251→            ?.let { parameterList ->
252→                if (!dryRun) {
253→                    emit(parameterList.startOffset, "No parenthesis expected", true)
254→                }
255→                if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
256→                    parameterList.treeParent.removeChild(parameterList)
257→                } else {
258→                    whiteSpaceCorrection -= parameterList.textLength
259→                }
260→            }
261→
262→        return whiteSpaceCorrection
263→    }

267→    private fun fixWhiteSpacesBeforeFirstParameterInValueParameterList(
268→        node: ASTNode,
269→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
270→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
271→        multiline: Boolean,
272→        dryRun: Boolean,
273→    ): Int {
274→        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
275→
276→        val valueParameterList = node.getPrimaryConstructorParameterListOrNull()
277→        val firstParameterInList =
278→            valueParameterList
279→                ?.children()
280→                ?.first { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
281→                ?: return 0
282→
283→        val firstParameter = firstParameterInList.firstChildNode
284→        firstParameter
285→            ?.prevLeaf()
286→            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
287→            .let { whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier ->
288→                if (multiline) {
289→                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null ||
290→                        !whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.textContains('\n')
291→                    ) {
292→                        if (!dryRun) {
293→                            emit(firstParameterInList.startOffset, "Newline expected after opening parenthesis", true)
294→                        }
295→                        // Let indent rule determine the exact indent
296→                        val expectedParameterIndent = indentConfig.childIndentOf(node)
297→                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
298→                            valueParameterList.firstChildNode.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(expectedParameterIndent)
299→                        } else {
300→                            whiteSpaceCorrection += expectedParameterIndent.length - (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier?.textLength ?: 0)
301→                        }
302→                    }
303→                } else {
304→                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier != null) {
305→                        if (!dryRun) {
306→                            emit(
307→                                firstParameter!!.startOffset,
308→                                "No whitespace expected between opening parenthesis and first parameter name",
309→                                true,
310→                            )
311→                        }
312→                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
313→                            whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.treeParent.removeChild(whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier)
314→                        } else {
315→                            whiteSpaceCorrection -= whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.textLength
316→                        }
317→                    }
318→                }
319→            }
320→
321→        return whiteSpaceCorrection
322→    }

324→    private fun fixWhiteSpacesBetweenParametersInValueParameterList(
325→        node: ASTNode,
326→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
327→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
328→        multiline: Boolean,
329→        dryRun: Boolean,
330→    ): Int {
331→        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
332→
333→        val valueParameterList = node.getPrimaryConstructorParameterListOrNull()
334→        val firstParameterInList =
335→            valueParameterList
336→                ?.children()
337→                ?.first { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
338→                ?: return 0
339→
340→        valueParameterList
341→            .children()
342→            .filter { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
343→            .filter { it != firstParameterInList }
344→            .forEach { valueParameter ->
345→                val firstChildNodeInValueParameter = valueParameter.firstChildNode
346→                firstChildNodeInValueParameter
347→                    ?.prevLeaf()
348→                    ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
349→                    .let { whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier ->
350→                        if (multiline) {
351→                            if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null ||
352→                                !whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.textContains('\n')
353→                            ) {
354→                                if (!dryRun) {
355→                                    emit(valueParameter.startOffset, "Parameter should start on a newline", true)
356→                                }
357→                                // Let IndentationRule determine the exact indent
358→                                val expectedParameterIndent = indentConfig.childIndentOf(node)
359→                                if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
360→                                    firstChildNodeInValueParameter.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expectedParameterIndent)
361→                                } else {
362→                                    whiteSpaceCorrection += expectedParameterIndent.length - (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier?.textLength ?: 0)
363→                                }
364→                            }
365→                        } else {
366→                            if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null || whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.text != " ") {
367→                                if (!dryRun) {
368→                                    emit(firstChildNodeInValueParameter!!.startOffset, "Single whitespace expected before parameter", true)
369→                                }
370→                                if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
371→                                    firstChildNodeInValueParameter.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
372→                                } else {
373→                                    whiteSpaceCorrection += 1 - (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier?.textLength ?: 0)
374→                                }
375→                            }
376→                        }
377→                    }
378→            }

380→        return whiteSpaceCorrection

392→        val closingParenthesisPrimaryConstructor =

411→                        } else {

413→                        }

417→                        whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.nextLeaf()?.elementType == RPAR
418→                    ) {

423→                                true,
424→                            )
425→                        }
426→                        if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
427→                            whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis.treeParent.removeChild(whiteSpaceBeforeClosingParenthesis)
428→                        } else {

430→                        }

437→    private fun fixWhitespacesInSuperTypeList(
438→        node: ASTNode,
439→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
440→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
441→        wrappedPrimaryConstructor: Boolean,
442→    ): Int {
443→        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
444→
445→        val superTypes = node.superTypes() ?: return 0
446→
447→        if (superTypes.first().elementType != SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY) {
448→            superTypes
449→                .firstOrNull { it.elementType == SUPER_TYPE_CALL_ENTRY }
450→                ?.let { superTypeCallEntry ->
451→                    emit(superTypeCallEntry.startOffset, "Super type call must be first super type", true)
452→                    if (autoCorrect) {
453→                        val superTypeList = node.findChildByType(SUPER_TYPE_LIST) ?: return 0
454→                        val originalFirstSuperType = superTypes.first()
455→                        val commaBeforeSuperTypeCall = requireNotNull(superTypeCallEntry.prevSibling { it.elementType == COMMA })
456→
457→                        // Remove the whitespace before the super type call and do not insert a new whitespace as it will be fixed later
458→                        superTypeCallEntry
459→                            .prevSibling()
460→                            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
461→                            ?.let { whitespaceBeforeSuperTypeCallEntry ->
462→                                superTypeList.removeChild(whitespaceBeforeSuperTypeCallEntry)
463→                            }
464→
465→                        superTypeList.addChild(superTypeCallEntry, superTypes.first())
466→                        superTypeList.addChild(commaBeforeSuperTypeCall, originalFirstSuperType)
467→                    }
468→                }
469→        }
470→
471→        if (superTypes.count() == 1) {
472→            if (wrappedPrimaryConstructor) {
473→                // Format
474→                //     class ClassWithPrimaryConstructorWhichWillBeWrapped(...) :
475→                //         SomeSuperTypeEntry
476→                // to
477→                //     class ClassWithPrimaryConstructorWhichWillBeWrapped(
478→                //         ...
479→                //     ) : SomeSuperTypeEntry
480→                superTypes
481→                    .first()
482→                    .let { firstSuperType ->
483→                        firstSuperType
484→                            .prevLeaf()
485→                            .takeIf { it.isWhiteSpaceWithNewline() }
486→                            ?.let { whiteSpaceBeforeSuperType ->
487→                                val expectedWhitespace = " "
488→                                if (whiteSpaceBeforeSuperType.text != expectedWhitespace) {
489→                                    emit(firstSuperType.startOffset, "Expected single space before the super type", true)
490→                                    if (autoCorrect) {
491→                                        firstSuperType.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expectedWhitespace)
492→                                    }
493→                                }
494→                            }
495→                    }
496→            } else {
497→                superTypes
498→                    .first()
499→                    .firstChildNode
500→                    ?.let { superTypeFirstChildNode ->
501→                        superTypeFirstChildNode
502→                            .prevLeaf()
503→                            ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
504→                            .let { whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier ->
505→                                if (node.hasMultilineSuperTypeList() ||
506→                                    classSignaturesIncludingFirstSuperTypeExceedsMaxLineLength(node, emit, autoCorrect)
507→                                ) {
508→                                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null ||
509→                                        !whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.textContains('\n')
510→                                    ) {
511→                                        emit(superTypeFirstChildNode.startOffset, "Super type should start on a newline", true)
512→                                        if (autoCorrect) {
513→                                            // Let IndentationRule determine the exact indent
514→                                            superTypeFirstChildNode.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indentConfig.childIndentOf(node))
515→                                        }
516→                                    }
517→                                } else {
518→                                    val expectedWhitespace = " "
519→                                    if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null ||
520→                                        whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.text != expectedWhitespace
521→                                    ) {
522→                                        emit(superTypeFirstChildNode.startOffset, "Expected single space before the super type", true)
523→                                        if (autoCorrect) {
524→                                            superTypeFirstChildNode.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expectedWhitespace)
525→                                        }
526→                                    }
527→                                }
528→                            }
529→                    }
530→            }
531→        } else {
532→            superTypes
533→                .forEachIndexed { index, superType ->
534→                    val firstChildNodeInSuperType = superType.firstChildNode
535→                    firstChildNodeInSuperType
536→                        ?.prevLeaf()
537→                        ?.takeIf { it.elementType == WHITE_SPACE }
538→                        .let { whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier ->
539→                            if (index == 0 && node.hasMultilinePrimaryConstructor()) {
540→                                val expectedWhitespace = " "
541→                                if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null ||
542→                                    whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.text != expectedWhitespace
543→                                ) {
544→                                    emit(firstChildNodeInSuperType.startOffset, "Expected single space before the first super type", true)
545→                                    if (autoCorrect) {
546→                                        firstChildNodeInSuperType.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(expectedWhitespace)
547→                                    }
548→                                }
549→                            } else {
550→                                if (whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier == null ||
551→                                    !whiteSpaceBeforeIdentifier.textContains('\n')
552→                                ) {
553→                                    emit(firstChildNodeInSuperType.startOffset, "Super type should start on a newline", true)
554→                                    if (autoCorrect) {
555→                                        // Let IndentationRule determine the exact indent
556→                                        firstChildNodeInSuperType.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(indentConfig.childIndentOf(node))
557→                                    }
558→                                }
559→                            }
560→                        }
561→                }
562→        }
563→
564→        return whiteSpaceCorrection
565→    }

567→    private fun classSignaturesIncludingFirstSuperTypeExceedsMaxLineLength(
568→        node: ASTNode,
569→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
570→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
571→    ): Boolean {
572→        val actualClassSignatureLength = node.getClassSignatureLength(excludeSuperTypes = false)
573→        // Calculate the length of the class signature in case it, including the super types, would be rewritten as single
574→        // line (and without a maximum line length). The white space correction will be calculated via a dry run of the
575→        // actual fix.
576→        val length =
577→            actualClassSignatureLength +
578→                // Calculate the white space correction in case the signature would be rewritten to a single line
579→                fixWhiteSpacesInValueParameterList(node, emit, autoCorrect, multiline = false, dryRun = true)
580→        return length > maxLineLength
581→    }

583→    private fun ASTNode.hasMultilinePrimaryConstructor() =
584→        findChildByType(PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR)
585→            ?.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
586→            ?.findChildByType(RPAR)
587→            ?.prevLeaf { !it.isPartOfComment() }
588→            .isWhiteSpaceWithNewline()

590→    private fun fixClassBody(
591→        node: ASTNode,
592→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
593→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
594→    ) {
595→        node
596→            .findChildByType(CLASS_BODY)
597→            ?.let { classBody ->
598→                if (classBody.prevLeaf()?.text != " ") {
599→                    emit(classBody.startOffset, "Expected a single space before class body", true)
600→                    if (autoCorrect) {
601→                        classBody
602→                            .prevLeaf(true)
603→                            ?.upsertWhitespaceAfterMe(" ")
604→                    }
605→                }
606→            }
607→    }

609→    private fun isMaxLineLengthSet() = maxLineLength != MAX_LINE_LENGTH_PROPERTY_OFF

611→    private fun List<ASTNode>.collectLeavesRecursively(): List<ASTNode> = flatMap { it.collectLeavesRecursively() }
612→
613→    private fun ASTNode.collectLeavesRecursively(): List<ASTNode> =
614→        if (psi is LeafElement) {
615→            listOf(this)
616→        } else {
617→            children()
618→                .flatMap { it.collectLeavesRecursively() }
619→                .toList()
620→        }

622→    private fun List<ASTNode>.childrenBetween(
623→        startASTNodePredicate: (ASTNode) -> Boolean,
624→        endASTNodePredicate: (ASTNode) -> Boolean,
625→    ): List<ASTNode> {
626→        val iterator = iterator()
627→        var currentNode: ASTNode
628→        val childrenBetween: MutableList<ASTNode> = mutableListOf()
629→
630→        while (iterator.hasNext()) {
631→            currentNode = iterator.next()
632→            if (startASTNodePredicate(currentNode)) {
633→                childrenBetween.add(currentNode)
634→                break
635→            }
636→        }
637→
638→        while (iterator.hasNext()) {
639→            currentNode = iterator.next()
640→            childrenBetween.add(currentNode)
641→            if (endASTNodePredicate(currentNode)) {
642→                break
643→            }
644→        }
645→
646→        return childrenBetween
647→    }
648→
649→    private fun List<ASTNode>.joinTextToString(): String = collectLeavesRecursively().joinToString(separator = "") { it.text }

651→    private fun ASTNode.hasMinimumNumberOfParameters(): Boolean = countParameters() >= classSignatureWrappingMinimumParameters
652→
653→    private fun ASTNode.countParameters() =
654→        getPrimaryConstructorParameterListOrNull()
655→            ?.children()
656→            .orEmpty()
657→            .count { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
658→
659→    private fun ASTNode.getPrimaryConstructorParameterListOrNull() =
660→        findChildByType(PRIMARY_CONSTRUCTOR)?.findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST)
661→
662→    public companion object {
663→        private const val FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY_UNSET = Int.MAX_VALUE
664→        public val FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY: EditorConfigProperty<Int> =
665→            EditorConfigProperty(
666→                type =
667→                    PropertyType.LowerCasingPropertyType(
668→                        "ktlint_class_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than",
669→                        "Force wrapping the parameters of the class signature in case it contains at least the specified " +
670→                            "number of parameters even in case the entire Class signature would fit on a single line. " +
671→                            "By default this parameter is not enabled.",
672→                        PropertyType.PropertyValueParser.POSITIVE_INT_VALUE_PARSER,
673→                        setOf("1", "2", "3", "4", "5", "6", "7", "8", "9", "unset"),
674→                    ),
675→                defaultValue = FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY_UNSET,
676→                ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue = 1,
677→                propertyMapper = { property, _ ->
678→                    if (property?.isUnset == true) {
679→                        FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY_UNSET
680→                    } else {
681→                        property?.getValueAs<Int>()
682→                    }
683→                },
684→                propertyWriter = { property ->
685→                    if (property == FORCE_MULTILINE_WHEN_PARAMETER_COUNT_GREATER_OR_EQUAL_THAN_PROPERTY_UNSET) {
686→                        "unset"
687→                    } else {
688→                        property.toString()
689→                    }
690→                },
691→            )
692→    }
693→}

47→     *     when {
48→     *         property == null ->
49→     *             // property is not defined in ".editorconfig" file
50→     *         property.isUnset ->
51→     *             // property is defined in ".editorconfig" file with special value "unset"
52→     *         property.sourceValue == "some-string-value" ->
53→     *             // property is defined in ".editorconfig" file with a value that needs to be remapped to another
54→     *             // valid value. For example the "max_line_length" property accepts value "off" but is remapped to
55→     *             // "-1" in ktlint.
56→     *        else ->
57→     *             property.getValueAs() // or null
58→     *     }

61→     * In case the lambda returns a null value then, the code style specific default value or the generic [defaultValue]
62→     * is used.

64→    public val propertyMapper: ((Property?, CodeStyleValue) -> T?)? = null,
65→    /**

67→     * default implementation in case you need a different behavior than the standard `toString()` (e.g. for

 10→private val LOGGER = KotlinLogging.logger {}.initKtLintKLogger()
 11→
 12→/**
 13→ * Loaded [Property]s from `.editorconfig` files.
 14→ */
 15→public data class EditorConfig(
 16→    private val properties: Map<String, Property> = emptyMap(),
 17→) {
 18→    public constructor(vararg properties: Property) : this(
 19→        properties.associateBy { property -> property.name },
 20→    )
 21→
 22→    private val codeStyle: CodeStyleValue
 23→        get() =
 24→            with(CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY) {
 25→                // Prevent printing a lot of warnings when the code_style_value is not defined explicitly by not using the function to get
 26→                // the default value for the property
 27→                type
 28→                    .getPropertyValue(type.name)
 29→                    .parsed
 30→                    ?: defaultValue
 31→            }
 32→
 33→    /**
 34→     * Gets the value of [editorConfigProperty] from [EditorConfig] provided that the name of the property is identical to the name of the
 35→     * type of the property. If the value is not found, the default value for the matching code style is returned.
 36→     */
 37→    public operator fun <T> get(editorConfigProperty: EditorConfigProperty<T>): T {
 38→        when {
 39→            editorConfigProperty.deprecationError != null ->
 40→                throw DeprecatedEditorConfigPropertyException(
 41→                    "Property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' is disallowed: ${editorConfigProperty.deprecationError}",
 42→                )
 43→
 44→            editorConfigProperty.deprecationWarning != null ->
 45→                LOGGER.warn { "Property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' is deprecated: ${editorConfigProperty.deprecationWarning}" }
 46→        }
 47→        val property =
 48→            properties.getOrElse(editorConfigProperty.name) {
 49→                throw IllegalStateException(
 50→                    "Property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' can not be retrieved from this EditorConfig. Note that the EditorConfig " +
 51→                        "which is provided to class '${Rule::class.qualifiedName}' only contains the properties which are defined in the " +
 52→                        "property '${Rule::usesEditorConfigProperties.name}'.",
 53→                )
 54→            }
 55→
 56→        editorConfigProperty
 57→            .propertyMapper
 58→            ?.invoke(property, codeStyle)
 59→            ?.let { newValue ->
 60→                // If the property value is remapped to a non-null value then return it immediately.
 61→                val originalValue = property.sourceValue
 62→                if (newValue.toString() != originalValue) {
 63→                    LOGGER.trace {
 64→                        "Value of '.editorconfig' property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' is remapped " +
 65→                            "from '$originalValue' to '$newValue'"
 66→                    }
 67→                }
 68→                return newValue
 69→            }
 70→
 71→        return if (property.isUnset) {
 72→            editorConfigProperty
 73→                .getDefaultValue()
 74→                .also {
 75→                    LOGGER.trace {
 76→                        "No value of '.editorconfig' property '${editorConfigProperty.name}' was found. Value has been defaulted to " +
 77→                            "'$it'. Setting the value explicitly in '.editorconfig' removes this message from the log."
 78→                    }
 79→                }
 80→        } else {
 81→            // In case the EditorConfig is loaded incorrectly it can happen that the type field of the loaded property is null. Getting the
 82→            // value via "property.getValueAs()" can lead to a class cast exception as the actual type of the value returned is transformed
 83→            // to a String instead of type T. Getting the value via the editorConfigProperty type is safe as it can not be null.
 84→            editorConfigProperty.getParsedValueOrDefault() ?: editorConfigProperty.getDefaultValue()
 85→        }
 86→    }
 87→
 88→    /**
 89→     * Gets the value of the property with [propertyType] and name [propertyName] from [EditorConfig]. Returns null if the type is not
 90→     * found. Also, returns null when no property with the name is found.
 91→     */
 92→    public fun <T> getEditorConfigValueOrNull(
 93→        propertyType: PropertyType<T>,
 94→        propertyName: String,
 95→    ): T? = propertyType.getPropertyValue(propertyName).parsed
 96→
 97→    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.getDefaultValue() =
 98→        when (codeStyle) {
 99→            CodeStyleValue.android_studio -> androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue
100→            CodeStyleValue.intellij_idea -> intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue
101→            CodeStyleValue.ktlint_official -> ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue
102→        }
103→
104→    /**
105→     * Checks whether a property with name [propertyName] is defined.
106→     */
107→    public fun contains(propertyName: String): Boolean = properties.contains(propertyName)
108→
109→    /**
110→     * Gets the parsed value of the property with type [PropertyType]. Returns null if the property type is not found. Can only be used for
111→     * properties for which the name of the property is identical to the name of the type.
112→     */
113→    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.getParsedValueOrDefault(): T? =
114→        this.type.getParsedValueOrDefault(this.type.name, this.defaultValue)
115→
116→    /**
117→     * Gets the parsed value of the property with type [PropertyType] and name [propertyName]. Returns null if the property type is not
118→     * found. Intended to be used for properties for which the name is not identical to the name of the property type.
119→     */
120→    private fun <T> PropertyType<T>.getParsedValueOrDefault(
121→        propertyName: String,
122→        defaultValue: T,
123→    ): T =
124→        getPropertyValue(propertyName)
125→            .let { propertyValue ->
126→                if (propertyValue.isValid) {
127→                    propertyValue.parsed
128→                } else {
129→                    LOGGER.warn {
130→                        "Editorconfig property '$propertyName' contains an invalid value '${propertyValue.source}'. The default value " +
131→                            "'$defaultValue' is used instead."
132→                    }
133→                    defaultValue
134→                }
135→            }
136→
137→    private fun <T> PropertyType<T>.getPropertyValue(propertyName: String): PropertyValue<T> =
138→        parse(
139→            properties[propertyName]?.sourceValue,
140→        )
141→
142→    /**
143→     * Maps all properties with given [mapper] to a collection.
144→     */
145→    public fun <T> map(mapper: (Property) -> T): Collection<T> =
146→        properties
147→            .values
148→            .map { mapper(it) }
149→
150→    /**
151→     * Adds the given [EditorConfigProperty]'s with its default value for the active code style to the [EditorConfig] if the property does
152→     * not yet exist.
153→     */
154→    public fun addPropertiesWithDefaultValueIfMissing(vararg defaultEditorConfigProperties: EditorConfigProperty<*>): EditorConfig {
155→        val editorConfigProperties = defaultEditorConfigProperties.toSet()
156→        requireSingularIdentities(editorConfigProperties)
157→        return editorConfigProperties
158→            .defaultProperties()
159→            .plus(
160→                // Overwrite default properties with values that were actually already defined in the EditorConfig
161→                properties,
162→            ).let { EditorConfig(it) }
163→    }
164→
165→    /**
166→     * Creates an [EditorConfig] containing [editorConfigProperties] only. Retains the value of the property when defined in the original
167→     * [EditorConfig]. If the property does not exist in the original [EditorConfig], it will be added and its value is set to the default
168→     * value of the active code style.
169→     */
170→    public fun filterBy(editorConfigProperties: Set<EditorConfigProperty<*>>): EditorConfig {
171→        requireSingularIdentities(editorConfigProperties)
172→        return editorConfigProperties
173→            .defaultProperties()
174→            .plus(
175→                // Overwrite default properties with values that were actually already defined in the EditorConfig
176→                properties.filterBy(editorConfigProperties.map { it.name }),
177→            ).let { EditorConfig(it) }
178→    }
179→
180→    private fun requireSingularIdentities(editorConfigProperties: Set<EditorConfigProperty<*>>) {
181→        val editorConfigPropertiesWithMultipleIdentities =
182→            editorConfigProperties
183→                .groupBy { it.name }
184→                .filterValues { groupedEditorConfigProperties ->
185→                    groupedEditorConfigProperties
186→                        .distinctBy { it.hashCode() }
187→                        .count() > 1
188→                }
189→        require(editorConfigPropertiesWithMultipleIdentities.isEmpty()) {
190→            editorConfigPropertiesWithMultipleIdentities
191→                .map { (name, editorConfigProperties) ->
192→                    editorConfigProperties
193→                        .joinToString(
194→                            separator = "\n",
195→                            prefix = "Found multiple editorconfig properties with name '$name' but having distinct identities:\n",
196→                        ) { "  - $it" }
197→                }.joinToString(separator = "\n")
198→        }
199→    }
200→
201→    private fun Set<EditorConfigProperty<*>>.defaultProperties(): Map<String, Property> {
202→        return associate { editorConfigProperty ->
203→            editorConfigProperty
204→                .writeDefaultValue()
205→                .let { editorConfigProperty.name to editorConfigProperty.toPropertyWithValue(it) }
206→        }
207→    }
208→
209→    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.writeDefaultValue() = propertyWriter(getDefaultValue())
210→
211→    private fun <T> EditorConfigProperty<T>.toPropertyWithValue(value: String): Property =
212→        Property
213→            .builder()
214→            .type(type)
215→            .name(name)
216→            .value(value)
217→            .build()
218→
219→    private fun Map<String, Property>.filterBy(editorConfigProperties: List<String>): Map<String, Property> =
220→        filterKeys { it in editorConfigProperties }
221→}
222→
223→private class DeprecatedEditorConfigPropertyException(
224→    message: String,
225→) : RuntimeException(message)

CHANGELOG.md:186:The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint `0.49`. Note that the default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` release.

54→            rule.beforeFirstNode(

56→                // the rule. In this way it can be forced that the rule actually registers the properties that it uses and the field becomes
57→                // reliable to be used by for example the ".editorconfig" file generator.
58→                // Note that also the CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY is provided because that property is needed to determine the default value of an
59→                // EditorConfigProperty that is not explicitly defined.
60→                editorConfig.filterBy(rule.usesEditorConfigProperties.plus(CODE_STYLE_PROPERTY)),

66→                e.line,
67→                e.col,

70→                Rule '${e.rule.ruleId.value}' throws exception in file '${code.fileNameOrStdin()}' at position (${e.line}:${e.col})
71→                   Rule maintainer: ${e.rule.about.maintainer}
72→                   Issue tracker  : ${e.rule.about.issueTrackerUrl}
73→                   Repository     : ${e.rule.about.repositoryUrl}
74→                """.trimIndent(),
75→                e.cause,
76→            )
77→        }
78→    }

111→                        FORMATTER_TAG_OFF_ENABLED_PROPERTY,
112→                        /*
113→                         * Used by [FormatterTags] to get the tag to enable the formatter.
114→                         */
115→                        FORMATTER_TAG_ON_ENABLED_PROPERTY,
116→                    )
117→            }
118→    }
119→
120→    private fun MutableMap<String, Property>.prettyPrint(normalizedFilePath: Path?) =
121→        map { entry -> "${entry.key}: ${entry.value.sourceValue}" }
122→            .joinToString(
123→                prefix =
124→                    "Effective editorconfig properties${
125→                        if (normalizedFilePath == null) {
126→                            ""
127→                        } else {
128→                            " for file '$normalizedFilePath'"
129→                        }

1→In ktlint `0.49` the new code style `ktlint_official` is introduced. This code style is work in progress but will become the default code style in the `1.0` release. Please try out the new code style and provide your feedback via the [issue tracker](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues).
 2→
 3→```ini
 4→[*.{kt,kts}]
 5→ktlint_code_style = ktlint_official
 6→```
 7→
 8→This `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This code style also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in those conventions and style guide.
 9→
10→!!! note
11→    Be aware that this code style in some cases formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor.
12→
13→The existing code styles have been renamed to make more clear what the basis of the code style is.
14→
15→* The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `official` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint `0.49`. Be aware that the default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` release.
16→
17→* Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
18→

1→Ktlint uses a limited set of `.editorconfig` properties for additional configuration. A sensible default value is provided for each property when not explicitly defined. Properties can be overridden, provided they are specified under `[*.{kt,kts}]`. Ktlint uses some properties defined by [.editorconfig](https://editorconfig.org/), IntelliJ IDEA and custom properties.
  2→
  3→!!! danger
  4→
  5→    Unfortunately [IntelliJ IDEA](https://www.jetbrains.com/idea/) has an [autoformat issue regarding `.editorconfig`](https://youtrack.jetbrains.com/issue/IDEA-242506). Due to this error an additional space is added between glob statements, resulting in `[*{kt, kts}]` instead of `[*{kt,kts}]`. The `.editorconfig` library used by `ktlint` [ignores sections after encountering a space in the list](https://github.com/editorconfig/editorconfig/issues/148). As a result, the rule is not applied on all files as documented in the [original ktlint issue](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues/762).
  6→
  7→## Code style
  8→
  9→By default, the `intellij_idea` Kotlin code style is applied. Alternatively, the code style can be set to `ktlint_official` or `android`.
 10→
 11→```ini
 12→[*.{kt,kts}]
 13→ktlint_code_style = ktlint_official
 14→```
 15→
 16→!!! note
 17→    The default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` version of ktlint.
 18→
 19→## Disabled rules
 20→
 21→!!! note
 22→    Support of properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` has been removed in KtLint `0.49`.
 23→
 24→Rule sets and individual rules can be disabled / enabled with a separate property per rule (set).
 25→
 26→All rules in a rule set can be enabled or disabled with a rule set property. The name of the rule set property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id. Examples:
 27→```editorconfig
 28→ktlint_standard = disabled # Disable all rules from the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
 29→ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable all `experimental` rules from all rule sets provided by KtLint or other rule providers
 30→ktlint_custom-rule-set = enabled # Enable all rules in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
 31→```
 32→
 33→Rules that are marked as experimental will not be run, unless explicitly enabled:
 34→```editorconfig
 35→ktlint_experimental = enabled # Enable rules marked as experimental for all rule sets that are enabled
 36→```
 37→
 38→An individual rule can be enabled or disabled with a rule property. The name of the rule property consists of the `ktlint_` prefix followed by the rule set id followed by a `_` and the rule id. Examples:
 39→```editorconfig
 40→ktlint_standard_final-newline = disabled # Disables the `final-newline` rule provided by KtLint
 41→ktlint_standard_some-experimental-rule = enabled # Enables the (experimental) `some-experimental-rule` in the `standard` rule set provided by KtLint
 42→ktlint_custom-rule-set_custom-rule = disabled # Disables the `custom-rule` rule in the `custom-rule-set` rule set (not provided by KtLint)
 43→```
 44→
 45→!!! note
 46→    The *rule* properties are applied after applying the *rule set* properties and take precedence. So if a rule set is disabled but a specific rule of that rule set is enabled, then the rule will be executed.
 47→
 48→
 49→## Final newline
 50→
 51→By default, a final newline is required at the end of the file.
 52→
 53→```ini
 54→[*.{kt,kts}]
 55→insert_final_newline = true
 56→```
 57→
 58→This setting only takes effect when rule `final-newline` is enabled.
 59→
 60→## Force multiline function signature based on number of parameters
 61→
 62→Setting `ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than` forces a multiline function signature in case the function contains the specified minimum number of parameters even in case the function signature would fit on a single line. Use value `unset` (default) to disable this setting.
 63→
 64→!!! note
 65→    By default, the `ktlint_official` code style wraps parameters of functions having at least 2 parameters. For other code styles, this setting is disabled by default. 
 66→
 67→```ini
 68→[*.{kt,kts}]
 69→ktlint_function_signature_rule_force_multiline_when_parameter_count_greater_or_equal_than=unset
 70→```
 71→
 72→This setting only takes effect when rule `function-signature` is enabled.
 73→
 74→## Wrapping the expression body of a function
 75→
 76→Setting `ktlint_function_signature_body_expression_wrapping` determines if and when the expression body of a function is wrapped to a new line. This setting can be set to value `default`, `multiline` or `always`. 
 77→
 78→When set to `default`, the first line of a body expression is appended to the function signature as long as the max line length is not exceeded.
 79→
 80→```kotlin title="ktlint_function_signature_body_expression_wrapping=default (or when not set)"
 81→// Given that the function signature has to be written as a single line function signature
 82→fun someFunction(a: Any, b: Any): String = "some-result"
 83→    .uppercase()
 84→
 85→// Given that the function signature has to be written as a multiline function signature
 86→fun someFunction(
 87→    a: Any,
 88→    b: Any
 89→): String = "some-result"
 90→    .uppercase()
 91→```
 92→
 93→When set to `multiline`, the body expression starts on a separate line in case it is a multiline expression. A single line body expression is wrapped only when it does not fit on the same line as the function signature.
 94→
 95→```kotlin title="ktlint_function_signature_body_expression_wrapping=multiline"
 96→// Given a single line body expression and
 97→// a the function signature that has to be written as a single line function signature and
 98→// it does not exceed the max line length
 99→fun someFunction(a: Any, b: Any): String = "some-result".uppercase()
100→
101→// Given a single line body expression and
102→// a the function signature that has to be written as a multiline function signature and
103→// it does not exceed the max line length
104→fun someFunction(
105→    a: Any,
106→    b: Any
107→): String = "some-result".uppercase()
108→
109→// Given a single line body expression then always wrap it to a separate line
110→fun someFunction(a: Any, b: Any): String =
111→    "some-result"
112→         .uppercase()
113→fun someFunction(
114→    a: Any,
115→    b: Any
116→): String =
117→    "some-result"
118→       .uppercase()
119→```
120→
121→When set to `always` the body expression is always wrapped to a separate line.
122→
123→```kotlin title="ktlint_function_signature_body_expression_wrapping=always"
124→fun someFunction(a: Any, b: Any): String =
125→    "some-result".uppercase()
126→fun functionWithAVeryLongName(
127→    a: Any,
128→    b: Any
129→): String =
130→    "some-result"
131→        .uppercase()
132→```
133→
134→This setting only takes effect when rule `function-signature` is enabled.
135→
136→## Ignore identifiers enclosed in backticks
137→
138→By default, the identifiers enclosed in backticks are not ignored.
139→
140→According to [Kotlin coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#names-for-test-methods) it is acceptable to write method names in natural language. When using natural language, the description tends to be longer. This property allows lines containing an identifier between backticks to be longer than the maximum line length. (Since 0.41.0)
141→
142→```kotlin
143→@Test
144→fun `Given a test with a very loooooooooooooooooooooong test description`() {
145→    
146→}
147→```
148→
149→```ini
150→[*.{kt,kts}]
151→ktlint_ignore_back_ticked_identifier = false
152→```
153→
154→This setting only takes effect when rule `max-line-length` is enabled.
155→
156→## Import layouts
157→
158→By default, the same imports are allowed as in IntelliJ IDEA. The import path can be a full path, e.g. "java.util.List.*" as well as wildcard path, e.g. "kotlin.**".
159→
160→The layout can be composed by the following symbols:
161→
162→*  `*` - wildcard. There must be at least one entry of a single wildcard to match all other imports. Matches anything after a specified symbol/import as well.
163→* `|` - blank line. Supports only single blank lines between imports. No blank line is allowed in the beginning or end of the layout.
164→* `^` - alias import, e.g. "^android.*" will match all android alias imports, "^" will match all other alias imports.
165→
166→Examples:
167→```kotlin
168→ij_kotlin_imports_layout=* # alphabetical with capital letters before lower case letters (e.g. Z before a), no blank lines
169→ij_kotlin_imports_layout=*,java.**,javax.**,kotlin.**,^ # default IntelliJ IDEA style, same as alphabetical, but with "java", "javax", "kotlin" and alias imports in the end of the imports list
170→ij_kotlin_imports_layout=android.**,|,^org.junit.**,kotlin.io.Closeable.*,|,*,^ # custom imports layout
171→```
172→
173→Wildcard imports can be allowed for specific import paths (Comma-separated list, use "**" as wildcard for package and all subpackages). This setting overrides the no-wildcard-imports rule. This setting is best be used for allowing wildcard imports from libraries like Ktor where extension functions are used in a way that creates a lot of imports.
174→
175→```ini
176→[*.{kt,kts}]
177→ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand = java.util.*,kotlinx.android.synthetic.**
178→```
179→
180→This setting only takes effect when rule `no-wildcard-imports` is enabled.
181→
182→## Indent size & style
183→
184→By default, indenting is done with 4 spaces per indent level. Code style `android_studio` uses a tab per indent level.
185→
186→```ini
187→[*.{kt,kts}]
188→indent_size = 4 # possible values: number (e.g. 2), "unset" (makes ktlint ignore indentation completely)  
189→indent_style = space # or "tab"
190→```
191→
192→Those settings are used by multiple rules of which rule `indent` is the most important.
193→
194→## Max line length
195→
196→By default, the maximum line length is not set. The `android` code style sets the max line length to 100 (per Android Kotlin Style Guide).
197→
198→```ini
199→[*.{kt,kts}]
200→max_line_length = off # Use "off" to ignore max line length or a positive number to set max line length
201→```
202→
203→This setting is used by multiple rules of which rule `max-line-length` is the most important.
204→
205→## Trailing comma on call site
206→
207→KtLint uses the IntelliJ IDEA `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site` to *enforce* the usage of the trailing comma at call site when enabled. IntelliJ IDEA uses this property to *allow* the use of trailing comma but leaves it to the developer's discretion to actually use it (or not). KtLint values *consistent* formatting more than a per-situation decision.
208→
209→!!! note
210→    In KtLint 0.48.x the default value for using the trailing comma on call site has been changed to `true` except when codestyle `android` is used.
211→
212→    Although the [Kotlin coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#trailing-commas) leaves it to the developer's discretion to use trailing commas on the call site, it also states that usage of trailing commas has several benefits:
213→    
214→     * It makes version-control diffs cleaner – as all the focus is on the changed value.
215→     * It makes it easy to add and reorder elements – there is no need to add or delete the comma if you manipulate elements.
216→     * It simplifies code generation, for example, for object initializers. The last element can also have a comma.
217→
218→Example:
219→```ini
220→[*.{kt,kts}]
221→ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site = false
222→```
223→
224→This setting only takes effect when rule `trailing-comma-on-call-site` is enabled.
225→
226→## Trailing comma on declaration site
227→
228→KtLint uses the IntelliJ IDEA `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma` to *enforce* the usage of the trailing comma at declaration site when enabled. IntelliJ IDEA uses this property to *allow* the use of trailing comma but leaves it to the developer's discretion to actually use it (or not). KtLint values *consistent* formatting more than a per-situation decision.
229→
230→!!! note
231→    In KtLint 0.48.x the default value for using the trailing comma on declaration site has been changed to `true` except when codestyle `android` is used.
232→
233→    The [Kotlin coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#trailing-commas) encourages the usage of trailing commas on the declaration site, but leaves it to the developer's discretion to use trailing commas on the call site. But next to this, it also states that usage of trailing commas has several benefits:
234→    
235→     * It makes version-control diffs cleaner – as all the focus is on the changed value.
236→     * It makes it easy to add and reorder elements – there is no need to add or delete the comma if you manipulate elements.
237→     * It simplifies code generation, for example, for object initializers. The last element can also have a comma.
238→
239→Example:
240→```ini
241→[*.{kt,kts}]
242→ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma = false # Only used for declaration site 
243→```
244→
245→This setting only takes effect when rule `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` is enabled.
246→
247→## Overriding Editorconfig properties for specific directories
248→
249→You can [override](https://editorconfig.org/#file-format-details) properties for specific directories inside your project:
250→```ini
251→[*.{kt,kts}]
252→ktlint_standard_import-ordering = disabled
253→
254→[api/*.{kt,kts}]
255→ktlint_standard_indent = disabled
256→```
257→
258→Note that the `import-ordering` rule is disabled for *all* packages including the `api` sub package. Next to this the `indent` rule is disabled for the `api` package and its sub packages.
259→

When using natural language, the description tends to be longer. This property allows lines containing an identifier between backticks to be longer than the maximum line length. (Since 0.41.0)
141→
142→```kotlin
143→@Test
144→fun `Given a test with a very loooooooooooooooooooooong test description`() {
145→    
146→}
147→```
148→
149→```ini
150→[*.{kt,kts}]
151→ktlint_ignore_back_ticked_identifier = false
152→```
153→
154→This setting only takes effect when rule `max-line-length` is enabled.
155→
156→## Import layouts
157→
158→By default, the same imports are allowed as in IntelliJ IDEA. The import path can be a full path, e.g. "java.util.List.*" as well as wildcard path, e.g. "kotlin.**".
159→
160→The layout can be composed by the following symbols:
161→
162→*  `*` - wildcard. There must be at least one entry of a single wildcard to match all other imports. Matches anything after a specified symbol/import as well.
163→* `|` - blank line. Supports only single blank lines between imports. No blank line is allowed in the beginning or end of the layout.
164→* `^` - alias import, e.g. "^android.*" will match all android alias imports, "^" will match all other alias imports.
165→
166→Examples:
167→```kotlin
168→ij_kotlin_imports_layout=* # alphabetical with capital letters before lower case letters (e.g. Z before a), no blank lines
169→ij_kotlin_imports_layout=*,java.**,javax.**,kotlin.**,^ # default IntelliJ IDEA style, same as alphabetical, but with "java", "javax", "kotlin" and alias imports in the end of the imports list
170→ij_kotlin_imports_layout=android.**,|,^org.junit.**,kotlin.io.Closeable.*,|,*,^ # custom imports layout
171→```
172→
173→Wildcard imports can be allowed for specific import paths (Comma-separated list, use "**" as wildcard for package and all subpackages). This setting overrides the no-wildcard-imports rule. This setting is best be used for allowing wildcard imports from libraries like Ktor where extension functions are used in a way that creates a lot of imports.
174→
175→```ini
176→[*.{kt,kts}]
177→ij_kotlin_packages_to_use_import_on_demand = java.util.*,kotlinx.android.synthetic.**
178→```
179→
180→This setting only takes effect when rule `no-wildcard-imports` is enabled.
181→
182→## Indent size & style
183→
184→By default, indenting is done with 4 spaces per indent level. Code style `android_studio` uses a tab per indent level.
185→
186→```ini
187→[*.{kt,kts}]
188→indent_size = 4 # possible values: number (e.g. 2), "unset" (makes ktlint ignore indentation completely)  
189→indent_style = space # or "tab"
190→```
191→
192→Those settings are used by multiple rules of which rule `indent` is the most important.
193→
194→## Max line length
195→
196→By default, the maximum line length is not set. The `android` code style sets the max line length to 100 (per Android Kotlin Style Guide).
197→
198→```ini
199→[*.{kt,kts}]
200→max_line_length = off # Use "off" to ignore max line length or a positive number to set max line length
201→```
202→
203→This setting is used by multiple rules of which rule `max-line-length` is the most important.
204→
205→## Trailing comma on call site
206→
207→KtLint uses the IntelliJ IDEA `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site` to *enforce* the usage of the trailing comma at call site when enabled. IntelliJ IDEA uses this property to *allow* the use of trailing comma but leaves it to the developer's discretion to actually use it (or not). KtLint values *consistent* formatting more than a per-situation decision.
208→
209→!!! note
210→    In KtLint 0.48.x the default value for using the trailing comma on call site has been changed to `true` except when codestyle `android` is used.
211→
212→    Although the [Kotlin coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#trailing-commas) leaves it to the developer's discretion to use trailing commas on the call site, it also states that usage of trailing commas has several benefits:
213→    
214→     * It makes version-control diffs cleaner – as all the focus is on the changed value.
215→     * It makes it easy to add and reorder elements – there is no need to add or delete the comma if you manipulate elements.
216→     * It simplifies code generation, for example, for object initializers. The last element can also have a comma.
217→
218→Example:
219→```ini
220→[*.{kt,kts}]
221→ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma_on_call_site = false
222→```
223→
224→This setting only takes effect when rule `trailing-comma-on-call-site` is enabled.
225→
226→## Trailing comma on declaration site
227→
228→KtLint uses the IntelliJ IDEA `.editorconfig` property `ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma` to *enforce* the usage of the trailing comma at declaration site when enabled. IntelliJ IDEA uses this property to *allow* the use of trailing comma but leaves it to the developer's discretion to actually use it (or not). KtLint values *consistent* formatting more than a per-situation decision.
229→
230→!!! note
231→    In KtLint 0.48.x the default value for using the trailing comma on declaration site has been changed to `true` except when codestyle `android` is used.
232→
233→    The [Kotlin coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coding-conventions.html#trailing-commas) encourages the usage of trailing commas on the declaration site, but leaves it to the developer's discretion to use trailing commas on the call site. But next to this, it also states that usage of trailing commas has several benefits:
234→    
235→     * It makes version-control diffs cleaner – as all the focus is on the changed value.
236→     * It makes it easy to add and reorder elements – there is no need to add or delete the comma if you manipulate elements.
237→     * It simplifies code generation, for example, for object initializers. The last element can also have a comma.
238→
239→Example:
240→```ini
241→[*.{kt,kts}]
242→ij_kotlin_allow_trailing_comma = false # Only used for declaration site 
243→```
244→
245→This setting only takes effect when rule `trailing-comma-on-declaration-site` is enabled.
246→
247→## Overriding Editorconfig properties for specific directories
248→
249→You can [override](https://editorconfig.org/#file-format-details) properties for specific directories inside your project:
250→```ini
251→[*.{kt,kts}]
252→ktlint_standard_import-ordering = disabled
253→
254→[api/*.{kt,kts}]
255→ktlint_standard_indent = disabled
256→```
257→
258→Note that the `import-ordering` rule is disabled for *all* packages including the `api` sub package. Next to this the `indent` rule is disabled for the `api` package and its sub packages.

622→    private fun fixWhitespaceBeforeFunctionBodyBlock(
623→        node: ASTNode,
624→        emit: (offset: Int, errorMessage: String, canBeAutoCorrected: Boolean) -> Unit,
625→        autoCorrect: Boolean,
626→        dryRun: Boolean,
627→    ): Int {
628→        var whiteSpaceCorrection = 0
629→
630→        node
631→            .findChildByType(BLOCK)
632→            ?.takeIf { it.findChildByType(LBRACE) != null }
633→            ?.let { block ->
634→                block
635→                    .prevLeaf()
636→                    .takeIf { it.isWhiteSpace() }
637→                    .let { whiteSpaceBeforeBlock ->
638→                        if (whiteSpaceBeforeBlock == null || whiteSpaceBeforeBlock.text != " ") {
639→                            if (!dryRun) {
640→                                emit(block.startOffset, "Expected a single space before body block", true)
641→                            }
642→                            if (autoCorrect && !dryRun) {
643→                                block.upsertWhitespaceBeforeMe(" ")
644→                            } else {
645→                                whiteSpaceCorrection += 1 - (whiteSpaceBeforeBlock?.textLength ?: 0)
646→                            }
647→                        }
648→                    }
649→            }
650→
651→        return whiteSpaceCorrection
652→    }

672→    private fun List<ASTNode>.collectLeavesRecursively(): List<ASTNode> = flatMap { it.collectLeavesRecursively() }
673→
674→    private fun ASTNode.collectLeavesRecursively(): List<ASTNode> =
675→        if (psi is LeafElement) {
676→            listOf(this)
677→        } else {
678→            children()
679→                .flatMap { it.collectLeavesRecursively() }
680→                .toList()
681→        }
682→
683→    private fun List<ASTNode>.childrenBetween(
684→        startASTNodePredicate: (ASTNode) -> Boolean = { _ -> true },
685→        endASTNodePredicate: (ASTNode) -> Boolean = { _ -> false },
686→    ): List<ASTNode> {
687→        val iterator = iterator()
688→        var currentNode: ASTNode
689→        val childrenBetween: MutableList<ASTNode> = mutableListOf()
690→
691→        while (iterator.hasNext()) {
692→            currentNode = iterator.next()
693→            if (startASTNodePredicate(currentNode)) {
694→                childrenBetween.add(currentNode)
695→                break
696→            }
697→        }
698→
699→        while (iterator.hasNext()) {
700→            currentNode = iterator.next()
701→            childrenBetween.add(currentNode)
702→            if (endASTNodePredicate(currentNode)) {
703→                break
704→            }
705→        }
706→
707→        return childrenBetween
708→    }
709→
710→    private fun List<ASTNode>.joinTextToString(block: (ASTNode) -> String = { it.text }): String =
711→        collectLeavesRecursively().joinToString(separator = "") { block(it) }
712→
713→    private fun ASTNode.hasMinimumNumberOfParameters(): Boolean = countParameters() >= functionSignatureWrappingMinimumParameters

715→    private fun ASTNode.countParameters(): Int {
716→        val valueParameterList = requireNotNull(findChildByType(VALUE_PARAMETER_LIST))
717→        return valueParameterList
718→            .children()
719→            .count { it.elementType == VALUE_PARAMETER }
720→    }

160→### API Changes & RuleSet providers & Reporter Providers
161→
162→This release is intended to be the last release before the 1.0.x release of ktlint. If all goes as planned, the 1.0.x release does not contain any new breaking changes with except of removal of functionality which is deprecated in this release.
163→
164→This release contains a lot of breaking changes which aims to improve the future maintainability of Ktlint. If you get stuck while migrating, please reach out to us by creating an issue.
165→
166→#### Experimental rules
167→
168→Rules in custom rule sets can now be marked as experimental by implementing the `Rule.Experimental` interface on the rule. Rules marked with this interface will only be executed by Ktlint if `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_experimental` is enabled or if the rule itself has been enabled explicitly.
169→
170→When using this feature, experimental rules should *not* be defined in a separate rule set as that would require a distinct rule set id. When moving a rule from an experimental rule set to a non-experimental rule set this would mean that the qualified rule id changes. For users of such rules this means that ktlint directives to suppress the rule and properties in the `.editorconfig` files have to be changed.
171→
172→#### EditorConfig
173→
174→Field `defaultAndroidValue` in class `EditorConfigProperty` has been renamed to `androidStudioCodeStyleDefaultValue`. New fields `ktlintOfficialCodeStyleDefaultValue` and `intellijIdeaCodeStyleDefaultValue` have been added. Read more about this in the section "Ktlint Official code style".
175→
176→The `.editorconfig` properties `disabled_rules` and `ktlint_disabled_rules` are no longer supported. Specifying those properties in the `editorConfigOverride` or `editorConfigDefaults` result in warnings at runtime.
177→
178→#### 'Ktlint Official` code style and renaming of existing code styles
179→
180→A new code style `ktlint_official` is introduced. This code style is work in progress but will become the default code style in the `1.0` release. Please try out the new code style and provide your feedback via the [issue tracker](https://github.com/pinterest/ktlint/issues).
181→
182→This `ktlint_official` code style combines the best elements from the [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html) and [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). This code style also provides additional formatting on topics which are not (explicitly) mentioned in those conventions and style guide. But do note that this code style sometimes formats code in a way which is not accepted by the default code formatters in IntelliJ IDEA and Android Studio. The formatters of those editors produce nicely formatted code in the vast majority of cases. But in a number of edge cases, the formatting contains bugs which are waiting to be fixed for several years. The new code style formats code in a way which is compatible with the default formatting of the editors whenever possible. When using this codestyle, it is best to disable (e.g. not use) code formatting in the editor.
183→
184→The existing code styles have been renamed to make more clear what the basis of the code style is.
185→
186→The `official` code style has been renamed to `intellij_idea`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of IntelliJ IDEA. This code style is based on [Kotlin Coding conventions](https://kotlinlang.org/docs/coding-conventions.html). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `intellij_idea`. When not set, this is still the default code style of ktlint `0.49`. Note that the default code style will be changed to `ktlint_official` in the `1.0` release.
187→
188→Code style `android` has been renamed to `android_studio`. Code formatted with this code style aims to be compatible with default formatter of Android Studio. This code style is based on [Android's Kotlin styleguide](https://developer.android.com/kotlin/style-guide). If `.editorconfig` property `ktlint_code_style` has been set to `android` then do not forget to change the value of that property to `android_studio`.
189→
190→#### Package restructuring and class relocation
191→
192→The internal structure of the Ktlint project has been revised. The Ktlint CLI and KtLint API modules have been decoupled where possible. Modules have been restructured and renamed. See [API Overview](https://pinterest.github.io/ktlint/api/overview/) for more information.
193→
194→This is the last release that contains module `ktlint-core` as it had too many responsibilities. All classes in this module are relocated to other modules. Some classes have also been renamed. See tables below for details. Classes that are left behind in the `ktlint-core` module are deprecated and were kept in this version for backwards compatibility only. The `ktlint-core` module will be removed in Ktlint `0.50.x`.
195→
196→Classes below have been moved from module `ktlint-core` to the new module `ktlint-rule-engine-core`:
197→
198→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-rule-engine-core`                               |
199→|----------------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
200→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.editorconfig               | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.editorconfig                            |
201→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigProperties     | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.EditorConfig                            |
202→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.OptInFeatures              | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.OptInFeatures                           |
203→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.ElementType                | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ElementType                             |
204→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.ast.package                    | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.ASTNodeExtension                        |
205→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.IndentConfig                   | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.IndentConfig                            |
206→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.Rule                           | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.Rule                                    |
207→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.RuleProvider                   | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.core.api.RuleProvider                            |
208→
209→Classes below have been moved from module `ktlint-core` to the new module `ktlint-rule-engine`:
210→
211→| Old class/package name in `ktlint-core`                  | New class/package name in `ktlint-rule-engine`                               |
212→|----------------------------------------------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
213→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigDefaults       | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.EditorConfigDefaults                    |
214→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.EditorConfigOverride       | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.EditorConfigOverride                    |
215→| com.pinterest.ktlint.core.api.KtLintParseException       | com.pinterest.ktlint.rule.engine.api.KtLintParseException                    |
